reviews.review_description: App Down. don’t work…
No please do not take off. I enjoyed it very much! Why take it down.
It says no internet indicated. We want to use it. Please fix.

reviews.review_title: Good selection of vids, limited quality
reviews.review_description: I like Within. Try it! But I also have to admit: The general problem is a  (current) technical limitation: The possible resolution. Even 4k Videos are very blurred. And imho 8k videos does not look much better. Because today, the resolution of the videos is far, far too low (My explanation: This resolution is projected on a globe around you. What resolution remains in your current field of view? When you see about 90x90 degree in your headset). And that's a pity. So, not Within is the problem. It's great. The current resolution of the videos destroys my "immersion". Reminds me of good old CRT TV: NTSC and PAL TV :-). So regarding content and the app itself: full points, regarding video quality one point. Average: 3 points.
Hello within is cool it would be a lot better if it was not so blurry!!
Please get the EltonJohn VR video on here

I really don't understand some of the other ratings (1 out of 5 stars!? Why…?).

I can't complain at all (like many others calling the app just "trash" for no reason). I enjoyed two short movies so far and they both were great: LEGO Batman – Batmersive Experience and Asteroids (see https://www.with.in/watch/the-batmersive-experience and https://www.with.in/watch/asteroids).

I like the simple and lean interface. No "Schnick-Schnack" as we would say in Germany.

By the way, The quality of the videos isn't worse than in the other VR video apps as far as I can say.

Also, I guess, none of the other VR video apps allows you to download the movies (Youtube 360, do you?). But, this one does!
Within even kinda encourages you to do so, the download button is right next to the streaming button. You can't not see it and the download is just a click away.
This is a great option! Since the Oculus is a portable device, offline-videos to show-off to your family and friends is the best way of introducing them into the world of VR.

Oh, wait. You get Within for free? – Alright, 5 stars! Nothing else to say.

A hub for 360 degree experiences, great.  The variety is nice and being that I spend a lot of time disconnected from the internet in my line of work it's handy.  That said, I've downloaded some of the independant experiences of comparable size in the past and for the same space, the experiences were trimmed down on content in some cases, and definately reduced in graphical quality.  I don't know how this app functions in relation to the content on other headsets, but I would recommend the developer experiment with compression going forward, so as to either reduce the size for the content by a paltry amount and/or to improve the presentation during playback.   I look forward to seeing this app grow along with the rest of the quest.

I just got Within on my Oculus Quest. However, when I go to open the app, it tells me it does not detect my network. Everything else works. It wouldn’t even let me go to the website on my browser. How can I fix this?

This is pretty neat and I was very happy to see The Legend of the Crow available through this platform. Graphics do leave a little bit to be desired. Anybody know if there’s recommendations on settings to optimize this app?
Has some decent videos, but I can't bring myself to give it above 1 star. This is a video app that you can't open with tracking off. A video app.

I won't harp on the quality of videos too much, as even Oculus's own video apps have terrible compression. 

I think this a great curated selection of experiences, especially for those who are new to VR. Unique stories and ideas being explored. 

App controls function well, though I and another person who tried it think the auto turn on the joystick moves waaaay to fast. I appreciate the option, but it's too vertigo inducing right now as is.

The experiences that focus on more live action realistic video suffer and downloaded or not often just aren’t sharp enough to be fully enjoyed. On the contrary there are several awesome animated and experimental art and sounds experiments that knock it out of the park. If you haven’t tried Crow or Inside the Sound (preferably with headphones) you owe it another try. WITHIN is fun!

reviews.review_title: Not bad but the video quality
reviews.review_description: The video quality is awe full
Pretty good portal for watching various VR/360 videos, especially the documentaries. Pretty good tool to introduce folks to VR as well - its like a curated YouTube channel!

my head set keeps stoping and the says  report to within 
thanks  love this one

reviews.review_title: Great content but video quality is bad
reviews.review_description: The content is great but the video quality is not good enough (even at maximum) and is holding back the app massively. I can’t immerse myself in grainy videos.

I am continuously delighted and surprised at the offerings available through this app.  Yesterday I was reliving my personal African baby elephant encounters.  Today I enjoyed watching what happened to a young man who decided to subvert the museum warning "do not touch."  I've listened to music and watched art created in NYC.  There is just no end to the creativity being offered, and stood in the audience of SNL.  Delightful.  Enjoyable.  Fun and entertaining.   And I even found a great pre-bed wind-down.
No idea why people are downvoting this, it’s a free app with very polished and inspirational videos - well worth downloading for some contemplation time

Nothing I queued to download would download. I left the headset for a few hours. Played games for an hour or two. I only saw progress while I had the app open and the headset on, but I'm not keeping it on my head staring at the progress for however many hours. I have terrible satellite internet, which is why I need to download videos in advance. I've seen other video apps download without having to wear the headset or keep the app open, like ecosphere
